journey.name: Admin endpoint authentication guard
journey.description: Verifies that every protected Admin API endpoint returns 401 Unauthorized when no Bearer token is supplied. Each step calls a distinct endpoint without an Authorization header — no step chains to another. The login endpoint is public and is excluded from this suite.

steps.name: GET /admin/users — no token
steps.description: Call the list-all-users endpoint without an Authorization header. The Admin API must reject the request before any downstream call is made.

expected.description: 401 Unauthorized — no Bearer token present. The middleware rejects the request before the handler executes.
expected.validationNotes: No user data must be returned. No downstream User MS call should be made.

steps.name: GET /admin/users/{userId} — no token
steps.description: Call the get-single-user endpoint with a well-formed userId but no Authorization header. The Auth middleware must short-circuit before the userId is resolved.

expected.description: 401 Unauthorized — no Bearer token present. The placeholder userId is a valid GUID format so the route matches; the auth guard fires before any lookup occurs.
expected.validationNotes: Response must be 401, not 404. A 404 would indicate the handler executed, which means auth was bypassed.

steps.name: POST /admin/users — no token
steps.description: Attempt to create a new user account without supplying a Bearer token. The request body is valid so a 400 cannot mask a missing 401.

expected.description: 401 Unauthorized — no Bearer token present. A valid request body is provided so only auth failure can produce this response.
expected.validationNotes: No user account must be created. No downstream User MS call should be made.

steps.name: PATCH /admin/users/{userId} — no token
steps.description: Attempt to update user profile fields without a Bearer token. A valid body is supplied so a 400 cannot mask a missing 401.

expected.description: 401 Unauthorized — no Bearer token present. No profile fields must be mutated.
expected.validationNotes: Response must be 401, not 404 or 400. Auth guard fires before route handler.

steps.name: PATCH /admin/users/{userId}/status — no token
steps.description: Attempt to change a user's active/inactive status without a Bearer token.

expected.description: 401 Unauthorized — no Bearer token present. Account status must not change.

steps.name: POST /admin/users/{userId}/unlock — no token
steps.description: Attempt to unlock a locked user account without a Bearer token.

expected.description: 401 Unauthorized — no Bearer token present. No account lockout state must change.

steps.name: POST /admin/users/{userId}/reset-password — no token
steps.description: Attempt to reset a user's password without a Bearer token. A valid new password is supplied so a 400 cannot mask a missing 401.

expected.description: 401 Unauthorized — no Bearer token present. No password hash must be mutated in the User MS.

steps.name: DELETE /admin/users/{userId} — no token
steps.description: Attempt to permanently delete a user account without a Bearer token.

expected.description: 401 Unauthorized — no Bearer token present. No user record must be deleted.
expected.validationNotes: Response must be 401, not 404. A 404 would indicate the handler executed, which means auth was bypassed.
